notes: Roman empress Faustina the Younger, wife of Marcus Aurelius, noted for her role during his reign and concern for their children.

docs.title: Correspondence, Book Volume 1
docs.author: Fronto / Marcus Aurelius
docs.excerpt: Of course you would have come had you been able. I am rejoiced that you can come now, and promise to do so, my master. Farewell, most delightful of masters. Marcus Aurelius to Fronto Lorium , 145–147 A.D.

docs.title: Life of Avidius Cassius
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: 8 So do not be anxious; 'Over me the gods keep guard, the gods hold dear my righteousness.'⁠ 37 I have named our son-in"law Pompeianus consul for next year."⁠ 38 Thus did Antoninus write to his wife.

docs.title: Letter CXVIII
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: … daughters, and that Faustina, most chaste and loyal of wives, your sister in the fervour of her faith and your one comfort in the loss of your children, has suddenly fallen asleep and been taken from you.
